posts.author: BuyHighSellLow
posts.community: Highness Microelectronics Ltd: a SMILE-type opportunity in electronics manufacturing?
posts.text: The story looks fine to me. But there are two big questions. Given the company’s presence in custom engineered and rugged display systems for industrial, medical, railway and defence applications, what is the strategic rationale for entering passenger automotive displays, which appears to be more competitive, price-sensitive, lower margin carrying (at least initially when they target Tier 2 and Tier 3 OEMs)? It would be helpful to understand the opportunity management sees in this segment, the intended customer profile, the expected qualification timeline and how this expansion fits alongside…

posts.text: “Centre’s Big Push To Manufacturing: Customs Duty Cut On Electronics Parts - Among the biggest beneficiaries are manufacturers of display assemblies used in automotive, medical and industrial equipment” - Source - https://www.ndtv.com/business-news/centres-big-push-manufacturing-customs-duty-cut-electronics-parts-make-in-india-11746965 This should be a big boost for Highness as the import 90% of their purchases and do value addition in India Screenshot 2026-07-09 at 10.44.38 AM 1496×1030 139 KB From RHP - image 1450×766 147 KB Disc - Invested

posts.author: bibhor
posts.community: Highness Microelectronics Ltd: a SMILE-type opportunity in electronics manufacturing?
posts.text: Management has indicated about 1.5 years for Phase-1 of Goa plant to go live . Very early stage . They have indicated that around 20 cr of capex would be required for both phases of Goa plant , Does anyone knows how do they plan to fund this capex ?
